title: crisp syrian grassy tagine
description: A comforting and dairy-free-friendly Syrian dish featuring the unique combination of dark chocolate and grassy herbs, served with a crispy crust.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, yeast, salt, and sugar.
In a separate bowl, whisk together water and olive oil.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Punch down the dough and shape it into a round or oblong shape.
Place the dough on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, brush with olive oil, and sprinkle with sesame seeds.
Bake the crust for 20-25 minutes until it's golden brown and crispy.
While the crust is baking, melt the dark chocolate chips in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in 30-second increments, stirring between each interval until smooth.
Once the crust is done, remove it from the oven and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Spread the melted chocolate over the crust, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Sprinkle the fresh rosemary and thyme leaves over the chocolate.
Let the tagine cool completely before serving.
